Home › ICU › Dr. Marty Makary Confirmed as FDA Commissioner, Ushering in a New Regulatory Era
Blog HHS

Dr. Marty Makary Confirmed as FDA Commissioner, Ushering in a New Regulatory Era

March 25, 2025
Dr. Marty Makary, a Johns Hopkins surgeon and former Fox News contributor, has been confirmed as FDA Commissioner, marking a major shift in the agency’s direction under the Trump administration. Known for his criticism of vaccine mandates, regulatory bureaucracy, and federal public health policies, Makary is expected to push for deregulation, faster drug approvals, and reforms in food and chronic disease policies.  Key Policy Implications
  • Vaccine & Public Health Oversight: A vocal critic of COVID-19 vaccine mandates, Makary has argued that public health officials ignored natural immunity and pushed vaccines without adequate advisory committee review. His leadership may result in tighter FDA oversight of new vaccines, particularly mRNA technologies.
  • Food & Chronic Disease Policy: Makary has aligned with HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in advocating for a stronger focus on nutrition and chronic disease prevention. He has claimed that ultraprocessed foods and additives contribute to rising health issues, though some nutrition experts argue this oversimplifies complex health challenges.
  • Drug Approvals & Industry Deregulation: Makary supports reducing red tape in drug approvals, allowing faster market access for new treatments. Critics worry that loosening regulations could compromise drug safety and oversight, particularly for novel therapies.
  • Reproductive Health & Abortion Access: As FDA Commissioner, Makary will oversee regulatory decisions on abortion medication, including mifepristone, which the Biden administration sought to make more accessible. His past comments on abortion have raised concerns among reproductive rights advocates about potential restrictions on medication abortion.
  • FDA Reform & Transparency: Makary has called the FDA “broken”, citing excessive bureaucracy and a lack of accountability in public health decision-making. His confirmation signals an effort to restructure the agency, potentially shifting regulatory power away from federal agencies and toward state-level decision-making.
Key Implications for Hospitals and Health Systems
  • Cost Reduction and Efficiency: Makary's focus on lowering healthcare costs may lead to initiatives that reduce unnecessary procedures and medical errors in hospitals, potentially resulting in new guidelines affecting hospital operations and resource allocation.
  • Drug Approval Process Changes: Advocating for streamlined drug approvals, Makary's approach could impact how quickly new treatments become available to hospitals, requiring hospitals to adapt their processes for adopting new medications and therapies.
  • Enhanced Safety Protocols: Strengthened oversight of clinical trials and safety protocols for approved drugs could lead to changes in hospital drug administration and monitoring practices, ensuring safer treatment environments for patients.
  • Pandemic Response and Supply Chain Considerations: Makary’s views on COVID-19 response and the FDA's role in drug manufacturing could influence future hospital guidelines on pandemic measures, while also prompting improvements in pharmaceutical supply chain resilience and inventory management.
Makary’s confirmation represents a significant departure from traditional FDA leadership, with a focus on industry-friendly policies, public health deregulation, and scrutiny of existing vaccine and drug policies. His tenure could reshape drug regulations, public health messaging, and access to medical treatments, with lasting implications for patients, providers, and the pharmaceutical industry alike.
